libraries.name: Webpage frames
libraries.description:
These components can be used while creating design for web pages to explain the concepts like
client side rendering, server side rendering , static site generators etc.

There are three version or states of webpages:

Loading - When Pages are being fetched.
Viewable - When HTML is loaded (painted) but JS in not executed yet.
Interactive - When HTML and JS are loaded and page is fully interactive.

libraries.name: GitHub Actions
libraries.description:
This library provides a set of essential items to streamline your GitHub Actions workflows.
 Whether you're tracking performance metrics, managing caching, or visualizing GitHub workflows, these items are designed to enhance your productivity and simplify your processes. 
Here's what you'll find in this library:

Metrics: Keep an eye on your workflow performance with clear and concise metrics visualization.
Cache: Represent caching mechanisms in your workflows with this intuitive icon.
GitHub Actions: A visual representation of GitHub Actions to help you map out and organize your automation processes.
GitHub: The iconic GitHub logo to represent repositories, projects, or the platform itself.

Perfect for developers and teams looking to optimize their workflow designs and presentations! 🚀

libraries.name: Japanese Pitch Accent Graphs Template Collection・日本語の高低アクセントのグラフテンプレートのコレクション
libraries.description:
Binary pitch accent graphs for illustrating Japanese pitch accent.

Blue = Flat accent -> Heiban
Green = Drop at the attaching particle (-1) -> Odaka
Red = Drop on the first mora (1) -> Atamadaka
Yellow = Drop between second mora and second to last mora (inclusive) -> Nakadaka

2 Varrations included, with and without mora count.

日本語の高低アクセントを説明するための二値のグラフ。

青＝フラットなアクセント→平板
緑＝助詞の前下がる（-1）→小高
赤＝最初の拍で下がる（1）→頭高
黄＝2番目の拍と最後から2番目の拍の間で下がる→中高

拍の数あり、拍の数なし、2つのヴァレーションが含まっている。

libraries.name: UML Component Diagram
libraries.description:
Different UML components used for making a component diagram. 
Component diagram shows components, provided and required interfaces, ports, and relationships between them. 
This type of diagrams is used in Component-Based Development (CBD) to describe systems with Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA).
Component-based development is based on assumptions that previously constructed components could be reused and that components could be replaced by some other "equivalent" or "conformant" components, if needed.
